reasoning: A vaccine for Colorado tick fever (CTF) does not currently exist, and there are no candidates in human clinical trials. Scientific papers from early 2024 and 2025 focus merely on reverse genetics systems and in silico multiepitope vaccine models, and the CDC confirms there are zero vaccines or medicines for CTF. As of May 29, 2025, Wikipedia listed this status as "No" (or an empty column functionally treated as "No"). The likelihood of a vaccine completing development, clinical testing, receiving approval, and being subsequently reflected on the Wikipedia article 'List of infectious diseases' by the end of 2025 is practically zero. The only factor introducing uncertainty is non-expert Wikipedia edits incorrectly citing theoretical research as an actual vaccine, or explicit vandalism, resulting in marginally higher probabilities towards the end of the year to reflect forecasting horizon uncertainty.
